comment: Exact label match between Person classes.
Exact label match between Document classes.
Exact label match between Review classes.
Exact label match between Tutorial classes; both denote tutorial events/lectures in conference context.
Exact label match between Session classes in conference context.
EKAW Location is semantically close to IASTED Place, though Place may be broader and more structured.
Conference participant in EKAW corresponds closely to delegate in IASTED, both denoting conference attendees/participants.
Presenter and Speaker are closely related roles centered on giving presentations, though not guaranteed identical in all contexts.
Paper author in EKAW closely matches Author in IASTED; both denote authors of conference submissions/presentations.
Possible_Reviewer is broader than Reviewer because it denotes someone eligible to review, not necessarily someone who actually reviews.
Labels differ only by underscore/case; both denote the chair of a session.
EKAW Student is broader than IASTED Student_lecturer, which is a more specific student role.
Abstracts are related to presentations in conference settings, but they are different entity types and not equivalent.
Conference banquet closely corresponds to dinner banquet; both denote banquet social events at a conference.
Conference trip is close to full day tour as a social/extra conference excursion, though exact scope may vary.
Social event in EKAW closely aligns with social program in IASTED, both covering social conference activities.
Proceedings are a kind of publication; EKAW Proceedings is narrower than IASTED Publication.
Conference proceedings is close to book proceeding; both denote proceedings publications, though modeling details differ.
Submitted paper closely corresponds to initial manuscript as the version submitted before acceptance decisions.
Camera-ready paper closely matches final manuscript as the finalized accepted version prepared for publication.
Accepted paper is related to final manuscript, but EKAW explicitly distinguishes accepted paper from camera-ready/final version.
Rejected paper is related to refusing manuscript, but the IASTED term appears to denote the rejection activity rather than the rejected document itself.
Review is structurally related to Reviewer through writing/reviewing relation, but they are not the same kind of entity.
EKAW hasReview property is related to IASTED Review class via paper-review association, but they are different entity types.
